title: Building AI Products + Intro to AI Agents
subtitle: Ship a real product in 50 minutes, build your first AI agent, then read production code
heroTitle: Building AI Products & Agents

objectives.title: Ship a Real AI Product
objectives.description: Build and deploy a working AI-powered product in 50 minutes using Lovable, Bolt, or Claude Artifacts.

objectives.title: Master Product Thinking
objectives.description: Apply the AI Product Canvas: Problem First, 10x Better, One Core Flow, Ship & Learn.

objectives.title: Build Your First Agent
objectives.description: Give Claude real tools and watch it reason about which to use. 25 minutes of hands-on coding.

objectives.title: Read Production Agent Code
objectives.description: Walk through NanoClaw's actual source — 700 lines that power 10,500+ GitHub stars. See the theory in practice.

agenda.title: Part 1: The Shift + Product Thinking
agenda.description: The world changed. You can build in hours what used to take months. Here's how to think about it.
bulletPoints: Opening hook: By 7:30, every team ships a product AND builds an AI agent
Stats: 70% faster prototyping, 10x more builders, $0 cost to start
4 Principles rapid-fire: Problem First, 10x Better, One Core Flow, Ship & Learn
LIVE DEMO: Build 'Resume Roaster' in Lovable from a single prompt

agenda.title: Part 2: Builder's Toolkit
agenda.description: Quick overview of your options. Pick your weapon.
bulletPoints: Tool categories: Chat-to-Artifact, Cloud Builders (Lovable, Bolt), AI IDEs
Decision Matrix: When to use what
Recommendation: Lovable or Bolt for today. Claude Artifacts for simple tools.
UX patterns you'll need: Set Expectations, Easy Recovery, User Control

agenda.title: Part 3: The 50-Minute Startup
agenda.description: Build, test, and deploy a real AI product. Teams of 2-3.
bulletPoints: 5:25-5:33 — Fill AI Product Canvas (problem, user, 10x advantage, core flow)
5:33-6:05 — BUILD PHASE: Prompt your tool, iterate, walk the room for feedback
6:05-6:12 — Test with another team, identify and fix the #1 issue
6:12-6:15 — Deploy + prepare 90-second pitch

agenda.title: Part 4: Demo Day
agenda.description: Gallery walk + top pitches. Celebrate what you built!
bulletPoints: Gallery Walk: Try every team's product (~1 min each)
Vote for favorites: Best Problem-Solution Fit, Most Delightful, Best UX
Top 3 teams do 90-second live demos
Quick peer feedback round

agenda.title: Part 5: From Products to Agents
agenda.description: The #1 trend in AI right now: agents that take action, not just answer questions.
bulletPoints: THE KEY INSIGHT: Chatbot answers questions. Agent DOES things.
The Equation: LLM + Tools + Reasoning Loop + Memory = AI Agent
The Agent Loop: Receive → Think → Execute Tool → Synthesize → Remember
NanoClaw vs OpenClaw: How 500 lines beat 400,000 (security + simplicity)
ACTION: Open Colab now, enter your API key while I talk

agenda.title: Part 6: Hands-On Agent Building
agenda.description: Build your first AI agent. Real tools. Real reasoning. Real magic.
bulletPoints: Single Tool (5 min): Give Claude a calculator. Ask '7,394 × 8,261' — watch it DECIDE to use the tool
Multi-Tool (8 min): Add stock + company info tools. Watch Claude chain them for complex queries
Experiment (12 min): Try your own questions. Build a custom tool for YOUR product
Key moment: Ask 'What's the capital of France?' — Claude knows it doesn't need a tool

agenda.title: Part 7: Production Code Walkthrough
agenda.description: You built a simple agent. Now see what production looks like.
bulletPoints: Open NanoClaw Code Walkthrough — real source code, annotated
index.ts: The brain — where Claude decides what to do
ipc-mcp.ts: The tools — how Claude talks to the outside world
db.ts: The memory — how Claude remembers across sessions
700 lines total. You could audit this in an afternoon. That's the point.

agenda.title: Part 8: Wrap-Up + Homework
agenda.description: The transformation narrative and what comes next.
bulletPoints: At 5:00 you used AI. At 6:30 you BUILT AI products. At 7:10 you built agents. At 7:22 you read production code.
You went from consumer → builder → architect in 150 minutes
Homework: Iterate your product, add a 4th tool to Colab, user test with 3 people, write 1-page brief
Next week: Data & Analytics — the fuel that powers every AI product and agent

resources.title: Agentic AI Deep-Dive
resources.description: Visual explainer: Chatbot vs Agent, the Agent Loop, and the NanoClaw case study. Perfect for review.

resources.title: Inside NanoClaw: Code Walkthrough
resources.description: Read real production agent code file-by-file. See how index.ts, ipc-mcp.ts, and db.ts map to the Agent Formula. ~700 lines that power 10,500+ GitHub stars.

resources.title: Agent Workshop Notebook
resources.description: Build your first AI agent with 3 tools: calculator, stock lookup, company info. Watch Claude reason about which to use.

resources.title: Lovable
resources.description: AI-powered full-stack builder. Describe your app, get working code with backend, database, and deployment.

resources.title: Bolt.new
resources.description: Instant web apps from prompts. Great for rapid prototyping and iteration.

resources.title: Claude Artifacts
resources.description: Create interactive tools, visualizations, and mini-apps directly in Claude conversations.

resources.title: Anthropic Tool Use Docs
resources.description: Official documentation for giving Claude tools. The foundation of building AI agents.

activities.title: The 50-Minute Startup
activities.description: Build and deploy a real AI-powered product in teams of 2-3:

steps.title: Fill the AI Product Canvas (8 min)
steps.description: Problem you're solving, target user, 10x advantage over alternatives, one core flow.

steps.title: Build Phase (32 min)
steps.description: Use Lovable, Bolt, or Claude Artifacts. Prompt, iterate, get instructor feedback walking the room.

steps.title: Test & Refine (7 min)
steps.description: Swap with another team. Get real user feedback. Fix the #1 issue.

steps.title: Deploy & Pitch Prep (3 min)
steps.description: One-click deploy. Prepare your 90-second pitch: Problem, Solution, Demo, Ask.

activities.title: Demo Day
activities.description: Showcase your creations and learn from classmates:

steps.title: Gallery Walk (8 min)
steps.description: Walk around, try every team's product for ~1 minute each.

steps.title: Vote
steps.description: Pick your favorites: Best Problem-Solution Fit, Most Delightful, Best UX.

steps.title: Top 3 Pitches (7 min)
steps.description: Winners do 90-second live demos. Celebrate all teams!

activities.title: Build Your First AI Agent
activities.description: 25 minutes in Google Colab — real tools, real reasoning, real magic:

steps.title: Single Tool (5 min)
steps.description: Give Claude a calculator. Ask: 'What is 7,394 × 8,261?' Watch it DECIDE to use the tool.

steps.title: Multi-Tool Agent (8 min)
steps.description: Add stock lookup + company info. Ask: 'Compare Apple and Microsoft market caps.' Watch Claude chain tools.

steps.title: Experiment (12 min)
steps.description: Try your own questions. Build a custom tool that would be useful for YOUR product from the build session.

codeBlock.code:
AI Agent = LLM + Tools + Reasoning Loop + Memory

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Chatbot: "What's our Q4 revenue?"          │
│  → "I don't have access to that data."     │
│  ❌ Dead end. YOU go find it.               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Agent: "What's our Q4 revenue?"            │
│  → *calls* query_database("revenue", "Q4") │
│  → *calls* calculator("2.3M - 2.05M")      │
│  → "Revenue: $2.3M, up 12% QoQ"            │
│  ✅ Agent did the work. You got the insight.│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

activities.title: Production Code Walkthrough
activities.description: You built a simple agent. Now see what a production agent looks like — NanoClaw's actual source:

steps.title: index.ts — The Brain
steps.description: Where Claude decides what to do. The reasoning loop that powers every action.

steps.title: ipc-mcp.ts — The Tools
steps.description: How Claude talks to the outside world. The MCP protocol that enables any tool.

steps.title: db.ts — The Memory
steps.description: How Claude remembers across sessions. SQLite keeps context without the cloud.

steps.title: The Takeaway
steps.description: 700 lines total. You could audit this in an afternoon. That's why NanoClaw won.

codeBlock.code:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  OpenClaw (Operator)                        │
│  • 400,000 lines of code                    │
│  • Closed source — can't audit              │
│  • Summer Yue's inbox deleted               │
│  • "Trust us" security model                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  NanoClaw                                   │
│  • ~700 lines of code                       │
│  • Fully open source                        │
│  • Audit everything in an afternoon         │
│  • Built by 1 dev in 1 weekend              │
│  • 10,500+ GitHub stars                     │
│  • Karpathy endorsed                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

"Simplicity beats features. Security is PMF."

takeaways.text: Ship fast, learn faster. A working prototype beats a perfect plan. You built a real product in 50 minutes.
Agents = LLM + Tools + Loop + Memory. This equation powers every AI agent in the world. You built one. You understand it.
The AI decides. You didn't write if/else logic. Claude REASONED about which tools to use. That's the breakthrough.
Production code is readable. NanoClaw's 700 lines beat OpenClaw's 400,000. Simplicity wins. Security is product-market fit.
Consumer → Builder → Architect. You went from using AI to building products to understanding production systems in 150 minutes.

homework.label: Iterate:
homework.text: Make 3 meaningful improvements to your product based on peer feedback. Document before/after.

homework.label: Build:
homework.text: Add a 4th custom tool to the Colab notebook that would be useful for your product. Test it with 3 different queries.

homework.label: Test:
homework.text: User test your product with 3 people outside class. Write down their exact feedback (don't interpret, just record).

homework.label: Write:
homework.text: 1-page product brief: Problem, Target User, Competition, Monetization Strategy, Next 3 Features.

nextSession.title: Session 5: Data & Analytics
nextSession.description: Every AI product runs on data. Learn how data flows, how to visualize it, and how it makes AI smarter.
